steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: An exponential model has the form $y = a \cdot b^x$. Two facts are given: (1) when $x = 0$, $y = 70$; (2) each increase of 1 in $x$ raises $y$ by 50% of its current value. We need $a$ (the initial value) and $b$ (the growth factor).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$y = 70(1.50)^x$': $a = 70$ matches the starting value; $b = 1.50$ matches a 50% increase each step. ✓
  • Choice B — '$y = 70(0.50)^x$': base 0.50 means the value halves each step — that is decay, not growth. ✗
  • Choice C — '$y = 50(1.70)^x$': initial value is 50, not 70. ✗
  • Choice D — '$y = 50(1.50)^x$': initial value is 50, not 70. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
